name: Tour around the Regional Park of Las Salinas y Arenales de San Pedro del Pinatar
name_original: Vuelta por el Parque Regional de las Salinas y Arenales de San Pedro del Pinatar
region_intro: Touring cycling around San Javier is characterized by its unique coastal geography, situated between the Mar Menor lagoon and the Mediterranean Sea. The terrain primarily features flat, fast routes along the coastline, with gentle inland climbs through agricultural areas. The region offers a mix of paved surfaces and unpaved segments, providing diverse options for cyclists. Best touring cycling routes around San Javier The most popular touring cycling route is Tour around the Regional Park of Las Salinas y Arenales de San Pedro del Pinatar, an 18.9 miles (30.4 km) trail that takes 2 hours 6 minutes to complete. This route explores the protected natural park, showcasing salt flats and coastal ecosystems. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Riegos de Levante Canal – Water Treatment Facility Wheel loop from La Ribera - Avenida Virgen de Loreto, 149, a moderate 32.6 miles (52.5 km) path. This route traverses varied landscapes, including agricultural fields and canal-side paths. Local touring cyclists also love the Rambla de El Mojón – Entrance to El Mojón Beach loop from Santiago de la Ribera, a 17.8 miles (28.7 km) trail leading through coastal areas and natural ramblas, often completed in about 1 hour 43 minutes. Touring cycling around San Javier is defined by coastal paths along the Mar Menor, flat agricultural plains, and routes that connect traditional villages. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy family rides to more challenging distances. The routes in San Javier are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 200 reviews. More than 1700 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore San Javier's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in San Javier?
A: San Javier offers a wide selection of touring cycling routes, with over 50 documented trails available on komoot. These routes cater to various skill levels, from easy rides to more challenging distances.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, San Javier has many easy touring cycling routes perfect for beginners and families. Approximately half of the routes are classified as easy. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40383667" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Pedro del Pinatar Promenade – Molino de la Calcetera loop from San Javier&lt;/a&gt;, which is 12.5 miles (20.1 km) long and features minimal elevation gain.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in San Javier?
A: The terrain in San Javier is diverse, primarily featuring flat, fast routes along the coast, especially around the Mar Menor lagoon. You'll also find gentle inland climbs through agricultural areas. Routes often include a mix of paved surfaces and unpaved segments, offering variety for different cycling preferences.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Many touring cycling routes in San Javier are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For example, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1016700987/tour-around-the-regional-park-of-las-salinas-y-arenales-de-san-pedro-del-pinatar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour around the Regional Park of Las Salinas y Arenales de San Pedro del Pinatar&lt;/a&gt; is a 18.9 miles (30.4 km) loop exploring a protected natural area.

Q: What are some scenic highlights or attractions I can visit along the routes?
A: Touring cycling routes in San Javier offer numerous scenic highlights. You can explore the unique ecosystem of the Mar Menor, Europe's largest saltwater lagoon, and visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/663908" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molino de la Calcetera&lt;/a&gt;, a historic salt mill. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5410964" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salt Flats and Sand Dunes of San Pedro del Pinatar&lt;/a&gt; are also a beautiful natural feature to discover.

Q: When is the best time of year to go touring cycling in San Javier?
A: San Javier benefits from approximately 320 days of sunshine annually, making it ideal for year-round cycling. The mild winters and warm springs and autumns provide comfortable conditions for exploring the routes. Summer can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended during those months.

Q: Are there any longer or more challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, for experienced riders seeking more challenging options, San Javier offers several moderate to difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40394691" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riegos de Levante Canal – Water Treatment Facility Wheel loop from La Ribera&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 32.6 miles (52.5 km) path traversing varied landscapes, including agricultural fields and canal-side paths, with more elevation changes.

Q: Where can I find routes that offer a mix of coastal and inland scenery?
A: Many routes in San Javier provide a blend of coastal and inland views. You can cycle along the picturesque Mar Menor coastline and then venture slightly inland through tranquil countryside and agricultural areas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33822672" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rambla de El Mojón – Entrance to El Mojón Beach loop from Santiago de la Ribera&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, leads through coastal areas and natural ramblas.

Q: What makes San Javier a 'Cycling Friendly' destination?
A: San Javier is recognized as a 'Cycling Friendly' destination due to its commitment to catering to cyclists. This includes an extensive network of over 90 documented trails, ongoing improvements to cycling infrastructure like new bike lanes, and a diverse range of routes suitable for all levels, from coastal paths to inland agricultural tracks.

Q: What do other cyclists say about touring cycling in San Javier?
A: The touring cycling routes in San Javier are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 200 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the beautiful coastal scenery, and the well-maintained paths that make for an enjoyable riding experience.

Q: Can I find routes that explore the Mar Menor lagoon?
A: Absolutely. The Mar Menor lagoon is a central feature of San Javier's cycling landscape. Many routes run along its 73 km coastline, offering picturesque views of the calm, warm waters. You can explore areas like Santiago de la Ribera with its beachfront promenade or cycle along La Manga del Mar Menor, the strip of land separating the lagoon from the Mediterranean Sea.

Q: Are there routes that pass through natural parks or protected areas?
A: Yes, San Javier offers routes that take you through beautiful natural parks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1016700987/tour-around-the-regional-park-of-las-salinas-y-arenales-de-san-pedro-del-pinatar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour around the Regional Park of Las Salinas y Arenales de San Pedro del Pinatar&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, allowing you to explore this protected natural space with its impressive beauty and unique coastal ecosystems.

Q: Are there any routes that include historical or cultural points of interest?
A: While many routes focus on natural beauty, some also pass near historical and cultural sites. San Javier itself has a rich history, with the town developing around a 17th-century shrine. You might find routes that take you through traditional villages or near landmarks like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/663908" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molino de la Calcetera&lt;/a&gt;, a historic salt mill, offering a glimpse into the region's heritage.
tips: The section near Santiago de la Ribera is a beautiful stretch along the coast of the Mar Menor, ideal for a bike ride. The route towards the salt flats of San Pedro del Pinatar offers a wonderful combination of beach, nature and fresh air. The route goes along small beaches, restaurants and charming cafes, with great views of the calm waters of the Mar Menor. It's worth it.
This section of road is still paved. After about half way to the mill this ends.

Excellent for cycling, scenic, with fantastic views of the water on both sides. The trip is well worth it!

The mill is located in the Salinas y Arenales de San Pedro del Pinatar Nature Reserve. It is one of the area's historic salt mills, which were once used to assist in salt production from the surrounding salt pans. These mills are an important part of the region's history and cultural heritage. Windmills like this one used to move water between the basins to facilitate salt extraction. Today, the mills stand as symbols of traditional salt production and are very popular subjects for photographs.

The Natural Park of the Salinas de San Pedro del Pinatar is a beautiful place near Murcia. Ideal for nature lovers that offers us an incredible landscape with good views and diversity of fauna and flora.

The area is enabled for cycling or hiking routes, as well as full of informative posters.

From here we can see both the Mediterranean Sea and the Mar Menor.

The mud of the ponds is the result of centuries of action of the sun and the very salty water of the Mar Menor. The result is a mud that, when applied to the skin, has great therapeutic value for all types of ailments: rheumatism, arthritis, gout, skin pathologies, rehabilitation after fractures, throat, etc. The ideal complement to the treatment is to take a bath in the mineralized water of the Mar Menor.
